original_abstract: This paper examines the formal and technical conditions for utilizing calcareous fly ash as an active pozzolanic-hydraulic agent in cements and binders. The study is supported by experimental results from hydraulic road binders and masonry cements formulated with calcareous fly ash sourced from the Bełchatów Power Station. Various mixtures were evaluated, including binders with varying concentrations of calcareous fly ash, as well as blended systems incorporating multiple combustion by-products, such as siliceous fly ash and fluidized bed combustion (FBC) fly ash. The research focused on assessing normative properties essential for hydraulic road binders and masonry cements. The findings demonstrate that calcareous fly ash from Bełchatów is a highly effective constituent, capable of producing high-quality hydraulic binders and cements that meet current industry standards and technical approvals.
humanized_abstract: Paper presents the formal and technical conditions for applying calcareous fly ash as an active pozzolanic–hydraulic cement and binder component. It has been illustrated using the examples of hydraulic road binders and masonry cements with calcareous fly ash obtained from the Bełchatów power plant. The research results obtained from the mixtures series were presented: fly ash mortars containing different amounts of calcareous fly ash and the mixes made with a mixture of fly ashes such as siliceous fly ash and the fly ash from fluidized bed combustion. The required by standards, normative properties of hydraulic road binders and masonry cements were checked. It was shown that the calcareous fly ash from the Bełchatów power plant is a very valuable component which enables the preparation of high quality hydraulic binders and cements satisfying current requirements and certificates.
